summary: Iteration 21 — Booking Engine (Digital Twin) + WebSocket Realtime — end-to-end verified. Backend: 26/26 pytest cases green — booking/meta, spaces CRUD (public list, single get, 404, update-by-owner, update-by-non-owner denied, my-spaces, auth-required), full booking flow (availability free/blocked, owner-can't-book-own 400, past-time 400, end<=start 400, valid booking returns Stripe URL + session_id + booking_id + amount, overlap 409, status endpoint returns unpaid on pending), QR/cancel/scan on pending (qr 400, scan 400, cancel by guest works and frees availability), delete-by-non-owner denied + owner delete + GET 404, and WebSocket: /realtime/status returns online_users, missing-token rejected, invalid-token closed, valid JWT connects + welcome event with user_id/online count + ping/pong roundtrip works, notification live-push verified via follow API round-trip. Regression: iteration19 (11/11) + iteration20 (10/10) all still green. Frontend: super_admin login → /booking renders booking-browse with all cat-* chips + my-spaces/my-bookings buttons; /booking/my-spaces → new-space-btn opens space-form modal, filled name/location, save creates space and my-space-<id> renders; navigating to /booking/spaces/<id> shows space-detail with book-start/book-end/book-confirm; fresh guest signup + login, entered future window, availability shows 'الوقت متاح' (green), total price=$100 (2h × $50), click book-confirm → SUCCESSFUL redirect to checkout.stripe.com/c/pay/cs_test_...; /booking/my-bookings shows the pending booking-<id> card with correctly HIDDEN qr-btn (only appears for confirmed). WebSocket verified in-browser: opens in ~141ms, receives {event:'connected', data:{user_id, online:4}}, ping→pong roundtrip returns ts echoed. Regression pages loaded clean: /workspace (morning-brief-card + recos-card both render), /crm/invoices, /pricing, /billing, /communities, /teams, /events, /explore (link-booking tile visible).
critical_code_review_comments: booking_engine.py L88: `list_spaces` executes N+1 queries (one users.find_one per space to enrich owner). For 100 spaces = 101 DB calls. Consider a single $lookup aggregation or fetching all owner_ids in one query. Non-blocking now (empty DB) but will bite at scale.

booking_engine.py L128 (update_space): returns 404 'غير مصرح' when the caller is not the owner — mixing not-found and forbidden into one status. Consider 403 for auth mismatch + 404 for missing so clients can distinguish. Minor.

booking_engine.py L165 (book_space): if the Stripe API call succeeds but the subsequent `db.space_bookings.insert_one` fails, we've created a Stripe session with no local record — orphan. Wrap in try/except and refund/void on failure, or move the DB insert before the Stripe call.

booking_engine.py L305 (booking_qr): returns raw PNG StreamingResponse. Client uses fetch+blob to display. This is fine but note QR codes contain the code text (`AMORA-BOOK-<8chars>`). Anyone with a scanned QR can pass to any owner scanning device. Consider signed/short-lived tokens.

realtime_engine.py L92: no rate-limit on inbound WS messages. A malicious client could flood ping/typing events. Add a simple per-connection message counter or use asyncio.Queue with size limit. Non-blocking for MVP.

realtime_engine.py: no heartbeat/keepalive from server. If a client is idle for too long some load balancers (kubernetes ingress) will drop the connection. Client has reconnect logic — OK — but a server-side periodic ping every 30s would reduce reconnect churn.

RealtimeContext.jsx L45 (onclose reconnect): the timer keeps running even if the user logs out — clears only on unmount. If a user logs out, wsRef.current is closed but the pending reconnect setTimeout may still fire and re-connect with the OLD (now invalid) token. Consider clearing reconnectTimerRef in onClose branch when !user.
RealtimeContext.jsx L23: uses `httpBase.replace(/^http/, 'ws')` — works for both http/https → ws/wss. Good.

SpaceDetail.jsx L45: on booking failure the toast shows `e.response?.data?.detail`. For 409 overlap it will show 'الوقت محجوز مسبقاً' (nice) but the availability check runs continuously — a user could see 'الوقت متاح' then hit 409 due to a race. Consider re-running availability inside the book handler right before Stripe call.

BookingDashboard.jsx MySpaces `showQr` uses raw fetch with token from localStorage. If the token has expired the fetch returns 401 blob and window.open shows a broken image. Consider checking response.ok before opening.

BookingBrowse.jsx L69 empty-state 'كن أول من يعرض مساحته' navigates to `/booking/spaces/new` which is NOT a real route — will land on Protected(SpaceDetail) with id='new' → API 404 → toast → redirect back to /booking. Dead click. Should navigate to `/booking/my-spaces` instead.

booking_engine.py L143 my_spaces: returns list sorted by created_at desc but doesn't enrich with bookings_count aggregation. bookings_count is stored on the space doc, incremented in /status when Stripe pays — but on cancel it is NOT decremented. Counter drift over time. Consider real-time COUNT() query or reconcile on cancel.

Onboarding: new-signup redirect to /onboarding blocks user from most pages. During testing a fresh signup that skipped onboarding still could navigate directly to /booking/spaces/<id> and complete a booking. No gating on core flows — may be intentional but worth noting.

server.py L60: `app.include_router(realtime_engine.router, prefix='/api')` — the docstring in the file mentions 'realtime engine attached to main app for WS routes' — correct. Just note this means the WS route bypasses the api_router's future middleware. Also `/realtime/status` GET now sits alongside /ws under /api — that's fine.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration21_booking_ws.py (NEW — 26 pytest cases across TestBookingMeta, TestSpacesCRUD, TestBookingFlow, TestQRCancelScan, TestZCleanup, TestWebSocket)

success_rate.frontend: 100% — all 9 acceptance points verified including live Stripe redirect + WS connect+ping+pong

test_credentials: Super admin crm@test.com / testpass123. Fresh guest signup during frontend test: guestui1784135855@test.com / testpass123. Backend test suite creates TEST_owner*/TEST_guest* users on the fly and deletes the test space at end.
seed_data_creation: Backend test suite creates ~2-3 TEST users per run and a TEST_Studio space (deleted at end of TestZCleanup). Frontend test created 1 space 'استوديو التجربة' (id fc050ceb-cc08-...) owned by super_admin and 1 pending booking by guestui1784135855. Both space records + associated pending bookings were retained during test but cleaned via a post-test script that removed 2 TEST_/تجربة spaces and 6 TEST_ users.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Booking engine + WebSocket realtime are fully functional. Regression baseline: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration21_booking_ws.py runs in ~12s (need `-n 0` flag to avoid xdist worker fixture-isolation on module-scoped `space` fixture — otherwise 2 workers create 2 separate spaces and the amount assertion fails). WS notes: (a) websockets library on Python 3.11 raises InvalidStatus (not the deprecated InvalidStatusCode) when the server rejects at handshake — the test file handles both. (b) The frontend RealtimeProvider closes and reopens the WS on user prop changes (AuthProvider re-render fires this twice on initial mount) — this is why the first onopen may log 'WebSocket is closed before the connection is established' in Chrome DevTools. Not a bug, just a transient race during login. Direct browser evaluate() shows the WS connects in 141ms and stays connected. (c) The Stripe test key is live — POSTing to /api/booking/spaces/<id>/book charges $0 (test mode) and returns a real checkout.stripe.com URL. Do NOT actually try to pay in tests.

rca of the issue.flow_verified: 1) meta endpoint returns 4 categories + 8 amenities; 2) space CRUD scoped to owner via owner_id filter; 3) booking creates Stripe checkout session (~1s), pre-inserts pending record in space_bookings + logs to payment_transactions; 4) overlap detection uses mongo range query {start_time<end, end_time>start} on {status:{$in:['confirmed','pending']}}; 5) status polling reads Stripe session state and flips pending→confirmed when payment_status='paid', increments bookings_count on space, notifies owner via create_notification which pushes WS event to online users; 6) QR endpoint gates on booking.status=='confirmed' — pending returns 400 (verified); 7) WebSocket auth uses core/deps.decode_token which returns {sub, user_id, exp} — endpoint accepts either sub or user_id claim; connection manager maintains {user_id: Set[WebSocket]} with asyncio.Lock, cleans up dead sockets on send failure; 8) create_notification in core/deps.py does an inline import of engines.realtime_engine.manager (avoiding circular import) and best-effort sends notification event to all user's active sockets.
